---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit README.Rmd and run `rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")`. -->

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
# ragg PNG + transparent background so GitHub does not show white behind
# transparent ggplot panel areas.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 2.2,
  dpi = 120,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  dev = "ragg_png",
  dev.args = list(background = "transparent"),
  fig.bg = "transparent"
)
dir.create("man/figures", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r pkg, include = FALSE}
library(whopals)
library(tidyverse)
```
<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- [![Check](https://github.com/finlaycampbell/whopals/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/finlaycampbell/whopals/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml) -->
<!-- badges: end -->

# *whopals: WHO Data Design in R*

This package brings the [WHO Data Design Language](https://srhdteuwpubsa.z6.web.core.windows.net/gho/data/design-language/) into R. Install it using:

```r
pak::pak("finlaycampbell/whopals")
```
## Palettes

Palettes are exposed as `whopals::pal_*` functions. Use the `theme` argument to specify a light or dark theme, `component` to specify the plot component such as text or base, and `variant` to select a palette variant where available. The available palettes are displayed below.

```{r plot-helper, include = FALSE}
# Dark canvas + white type: readable on GitHub; knitr uses transparent device bg.
plot_pal <- function(x) {
  lab <- names(x)
  if (is.null(lab)) {
    lab <- as.character(seq_along(x))
  }
  df <- data.frame(
    label = factor(lab, levels = lab),
    y = 1L,
    col = unname(x),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  ggplot(df, aes(x = label, y = y, fill = col)) +
    geom_tile(width = 0.92, height = 0.85) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, caption = NULL, tag = NULL) +
    theme_void(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(
        angle = 35,
        hjust = 1,
        vjust = 1,
        color = "white",
        size = 9,
        margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 10, unit = "pt")
      ),
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      axis.line = element_blank(),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_blank(),
      plot.subtitle = element_blank(),
      plot.caption = element_blank(),
      plot.tag = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(6, 8, 22, 8),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", linewidth = 0),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#0d1117", color = NA),
      panel.border = element_blank()
    )
}
```

**`pal_region()`**

```{r pal-region}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_region(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_trend()`**

```{r pal-trend}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_trend(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_gender()`**

```{r pal-gender}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_gender("dark"))
```

**`pal_category()`**

```{r pal-category}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_category(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_sequential("brand")`**

```{r pal-sequential-brand, fig.height = 2.2}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_sequential("brand"))
```

**`pal_sequential("complementary")`**

```{r pal-sequential-complementary, fig.height = 2.2}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_sequential("complementary"))
```

**`pal_sequential("colorful")`**

```{r pal-sequential-colorful, fig.height = 2.2}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_sequential("colorful"))
```

**`pal_diverging("base")`**

```{r pal-diverging-base, fig.height = 2.2}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_diverging("base", theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_diverging("alt")`**

```{r pal-diverging-alt, fig.height = 2.2}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_diverging("alt", theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_brand()`**

```{r pal-brand, fig.height = 1.5}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_brand(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_foreground()`**

```{r pal-foreground, fig.height = 1.5}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_foreground(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_background()`**

```{r pal-background, fig.height = 1.5}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_background(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_text()`**

```{r pal-text, fig.height = 1.5}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_text(theme = "dark"))
```

**`pal_functional()`**

```{r pal-functional, fig.height = 3.2}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_functional("dark"))
```

**`pal_selection()`**

```{r pal-selection, fig.height = 2.8}
plot_pal(whopals::pal_selection(theme = "dark"))
```

## Themes

The basic design guidelines are implemented as `ggplot2` themes and displayed below.
	
```{r example-col-data, echo=FALSE}
df <- tibble(
  who_region = c("afro", "amro", "emro", "euro", "searo", "wpro"),
  data = replicate(
    length(who_region),
    tibble(
      day = ymd("2026-01-01") + seq_len(100),
      cases = map(
        exp(cumsum(seq(runif(1, 0.05, 0.1), runif(1, -0.02, 0.02), length = 100))),
        ~ quantile(rpois(100, .x), c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)) |>
          setNames(c("lower", "mid", "upper"))
      )
    ),
    simplify = FALSE
  )
) |>
  unnest(data) |>
  unnest_wider(cases)
```

**`theme_who_dark()`**

```{r example-col-dark, echo=FALSE, fig.height = 4.2, fig.width = 8}
p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = day)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = who_region), alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = mid, color = who_region)) +  
  facet_wrap(
    ~ who_region,
    scales = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(who_region = toupper)
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_region()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_region()) +
  labs(
    title = "SARS-CoV-2 Incidence",
    x = "Date of reporting",
    y = "Daily number of cases"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  scale_x_date(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  theme_who_dark() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
p
```

**`theme_who_light()`**

```{r example-col-light, echo=FALSE, fig.height = 4.2, fig.width = 8}
p +
  theme_who_light() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Fonts

The WHO Data Design Language recommends use of the [Noto Sans](https://fonts.google.com/noto/specimen/Noto+Sans) font ([download here](https://fonts.google.com/download?family=Noto%20Sans)). After unzipping, the files can be installed by right-mouse click: `Install` on the `ttf` files. This font will be automatically used by the `theme_who_*` functions if available.

```{r fonts-specimen, echo=FALSE, fig.height = 1.35, fig.width = 7}
lor <- paste(
  "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit,",
  "tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua."
)
has_noto <- isTRUE(
  tryCatch(
    any(
      tolower(systemfonts::system_fonts()$family) == "noto sans",
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) FALSE
  )
)
fam <- if (has_noto) "Noto Sans" else ""
txt_col <- unname(whopals::pal_text(theme = "dark"))
ggplot2::ggplot() +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 1),
    ylim = c(0.06, 1),
    expand = FALSE,
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02,
    y = 0.97,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    family = fam,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 5.5,
    colour = txt_col,
    label = "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02,
    y = 0.78,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    family = fam,
    fontface = "plain",
    size = 4.2,
    colour = txt_col,
    label = lor
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02,
    y = 0.58,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    family = fam,
    fontface = "italic",
    size = 3.8,
    colour = txt_col,
    label = lor
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02,
    y = 0.40,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    family = fam,
    fontface = "bold.italic",
    size = 3.4,
    colour = txt_col,
    label = "Lorem ipsum"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.02,
    y = 0.24,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    family = fam,
    fontface = "plain",
    size = 2.6,
    colour = txt_col,
    label = "ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris."
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_void() +
  ggplot2::theme(plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(8, 12, 2, 12))
```

## Saving

The Noto Sans font cannot be rendered using default devices and requires `ragg::agg_*` as the device for non-vectorised images or `grDevices::cairo_pdf` for vectorised images. This functionality is implemented within function `save_plot`, which acts as a wrapper for `ggplot2::ggsave` with sensible defaults.
